title: First married gay couple wins US green card approval
description: Julian Marsh and Traian Povov became the first married same-sex couple to have a green card application approved by US immigration authorities, following the Supreme Court's DOMA ruling.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Air Force grants first joint assignment to a same-sex military couple
description: Master Sgt. Angela Shunk and her wife, Tech. Sgt. Stacey Shunk, received a joint posting through the US Air Force's Join Spouse program, the first same-sex couple to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex kiss on a Eurovision stage
description: Finnish singer Krista Siegfrids closed her semi-final performance of "Marry Me" at the 2013 Eurovision Song Contest in Malmo by kissing one of her female dancers, the contest's first on-stage same-sex kiss.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Guy Erwin elected first openly gay Lutheran bishop in the US
description: The Evangelical Lutheran Church in America elected Guy Erwin bishop of its Southwest California Synod, the denomination's first openly gay bishop.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Bisexual Book Awards presented in New York
description: The Bi Writers Association announced the winners of its first Bisexual Book Awards, honoring bisexual writers and books, with a ceremony at the Nuyorican Poets Cafe in New York City.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Paris Barclay becomes first openly gay president of the Directors Guild
description: The Directors Guild of America elected Paris Barclay as its president, the first Black person and first openly gay person to hold the office.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: "Same Love" becomes first US Top 40 hit endorsing same-sex marriage
description: The Macklemore & Ryan Lewis single "Same Love" reached the US Top 40, the first song at that chart level to openly support same-sex marriage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First public same-sex wedding held in South Korea
description: Film director Kim Jho Gwang-soo and his partner Kim Seung-hwan held South Korea's first public same-sex wedding ceremony; the marriage had no legal recognition.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Harvey Milk selected for a US postage stamp
description: Harvey Milk was chosen to appear on a US postage stamp, the first openly LGBT elected official to receive the honor.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andy Herren wins US Big Brother as first openly gay champion
description: Andy Herren won the American edition of the reality show Big Brother, its first openly gay winner.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex wedding shown on Romanian television
description: Two men held Romania's first televised same-sex wedding on the reality show Four Weddings and a Challenge.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Q Radio launches as India's first LGBT radio station
description: Q Radio went on air in September 2013, describing itself as India's first radio station aimed at lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender listeners.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: American Conference of Cantors elects first openly gay president
description: Mark C. Goldman became the first openly gay president of the American Conference of Cantors, a Reform Jewish body.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Deborah Waxman elected to lead the Reconstructionist Rabbinical College
description: Rabbi Deborah Waxman was elected president of the Reconstructionist Rabbinical College, regarded as the first woman and first lesbian to head both a Jewish congregational union and a Jewish seminary.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Saul Levin named first openly gay head of the American Psychiatric Association
description: On 15 May 2013 Saul Levin was appointed chief executive officer and medical director of the American Psychiatric Association, the first known openly gay person to lead the organization.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Patricia Rose becomes highest-ranking openly gay US officer
description: Major General Patricia Rose of the US Air Force became the first openly lesbian two-star general and, at the time, the highest-ranking openly gay officer in the US military.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: New Jersey bans conversion therapy for minors
description: New Jersey outlawed therapy claiming to change gay people's sexual orientation, becoming the second US state to do so after California.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Russia enacts federal "gay propaganda" law
description: Russia adopted a federal law banning the distribution of "propaganda of non-traditional sexual relations" to minors, imposing heavy fines on media and internet advocacy of LGBT topics.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco holds first LGBT Project Homeless Connect
description: San Francisco held its first Project Homeless Connect event dedicated to lesbian, gay, bisexual and transgender people experiencing homelessness.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: BiLaw, first US national association of bisexual lawyers, forms
description: BiLaw was established as the first American national organization bringing together bisexual lawyers, law professors, law students and allies.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Scotland
description: Scotland's same-sex marriage legislation passed and took effect in 2014, extending marriage rights to same-sex couples across the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage arrives in nineteen more US states
description: During 2014 same-sex marriage became legal in Oregon, Pennsylvania, Utah, Oklahoma, Virginia, Wisconsin, Indiana, Colorado, Nevada, Idaho, West Virginia, North Carolina, Alaska, Arizona, Wyoming, Kansas, South Carolina and Montana, largely through court rulings striking down state bans.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Native American tribal nations adopt same-sex marriage
description: In 2014 several sovereign tribal nations in the United States legalized same-sex marriage, including the Puyallup Tribe, the Pascua Yaqui Tribe, the Salt River Pima-Maricopa Indian Community, the San Carlos Apache Tribe, the Blackfoot Tribe and others.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Coahuila legalizes same-sex marriage
description: The Mexican state of Coahuila passed and put into effect a law opening marriage to same-sex couples in 2014.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Luxembourg passes same-sex marriage law
description: Luxembourg's parliament approved legislation opening marriage to same-sex couples in 2014.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in England and Wales
description: The same-sex marriage law for England and Wales, passed in 2013, came into force in 2014, allowing the first weddings of same-sex couples there.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Same-sex marriage takes effect in Illinois
description: Illinois's same-sex marriage law came into force in 2014, making marriage available to same-sex couples statewide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Gibraltar introduces civil partnerships with joint adoption
description: Gibraltar, a British overseas territory, passed and brought into force a civil partnership law that includes joint adoption rights for same-sex couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Malta enacts civil unions with joint adoption
description: Malta passed and brought into force a civil union law granting same-sex couples marriage-equivalent rights, including joint adoption.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Croatia adopts life partnership law for same-sex couples
description: Croatia passed and brought into force a registered life partnership law for same-sex couples in 2014.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Andorra allows same-sex couples to adopt
description: Andorra passed legislation in 2014 permitting same-sex couples to adopt children.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Coahuila grants adoption rights to same-sex couples
description: The Mexican state of Coahuila enacted legislation in 2014 allowing same-sex couples to adopt children.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Northern Cyprus decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Northern Cyprus repealed its criminal ban on same-sex acts in 2014, the last territory in Europe to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Palau decriminalizes homosexuality
description: Palau repealed its law criminalizing consensual same-sex acts in 2014.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Brunei criminalizes homosexuality under new penal code
description: Brunei introduced a penal code in 2014 that criminalizes same-sex relations, sharply worsening the legal situation of LGBTQ people in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Nigeria outlaws same-sex marriage
description: Nigeria enacted a law in 2014 banning same-sex marriage, part of legislation that also penalizes same-sex relationships and LGBTQ advocacy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Saskatchewan adds gender identity to anti-discrimination law
description: The Canadian province of Saskatchewan amended its human rights legislation in 2014 to prohibit discrimination based on gender identity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First same-sex marriage entered into public record in Mississippi
description: Anna Guillot and Chrissy Kelly, married in New York in 2012, became the first same-sex couple to place their marriage on public record in Mississippi, though the state did not recognize it legally.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Italy legally recognizes a foreign same-sex marriage for the first time
description: The marriage of Giuseppe Chigiotti and Stefano Bucci, performed in New York in 2012, became the first overseas same-sex marriage to receive legal recognition in Italy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Italian court approves first adoption within a same-sex couple
description: An Italian court for the first time allowed the partner of a child's biological mother to adopt the girl, letting both women in the couple become legal parents.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: World's first cemetery for lesbians opens in Berlin
description: A burial ground dedicated to lesbians, described as the first of its kind worldwide, was unveiled in Berlin.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Catholic Church in Argentina baptizes child of a lesbian couple
description: Umma Azul became the first child of a lesbian couple to be baptized by the Catholic Church in Argentina.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tennessee birth certificate lists a woman as "father" for the first time
description: Emilia Maria Jesty, daughter of a lesbian couple, became the first child born in Tennessee whose birth certificate listed a woman in the "father" field.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US grants survivor benefits to first known same-sex war widow
description: The US Department of Veterans Affairs agreed to pay survivor benefits to Tracy Dice Johnson, whose wife Donna Johnson was killed in a 2012 suicide bombing, the first known same-sex war widow to receive them.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Australian birth certificate names both mothers as legal parents
description: For the first time in Australia, a birth certificate recorded both women of a same-sex couple as the legal parents of their children at birth.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US Naval Academy Chapel hosts its first same-sex wedding
description: Academy graduate David Bucher and his partner Bruce Moats were married in the US Naval Academy Chapel, the first same-sex wedding held there.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US government files first Title VII suits for transgender workers
description: The US Equal Employment Opportunity Commission filed its first Title VII lawsuits on behalf of transgender employees, acting for Aimee Stephens and Brandi Branson.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Meghan Stabler named Working Mother of the Year
description: Meghan Stabler became the first openly transgender woman to receive Working Mother magazine's Working Mother of the Year award.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Laverne Cox lands Time cover and a first transgender Emmy nomination
description: Laverne Cox appeared on the cover of Time's 9 June 2014 issue for "The Transgender Tipping Point" and became the first openly transgender person nominated for an acting Emmy, for her role in Orange Is the New Black.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Transgender Studies Quarterly begins publication
description: Transgender Studies Quarterly, the first non-medical academic journal devoted to transgender issues, published its first issue with Susan Stryker and Paisley Currah as coeditors.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Mills College and Mount Holyoke open admission to transgender students
description: Mills College became the first single-sex US college to adopt a policy explicitly welcoming transgender students, and Mount Holyoke became the first of the Seven Sisters colleges to admit them.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Blake Brockington crowned North Carolina's first trans homecoming king
description: Blake Brockington became the first openly transgender student to be named high school homecoming king in North Carolina.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Trans Lifeline, first US suicide hotline for trans people, launches
description: Nina Chaubal and Greta Gustava Martela cofounded Trans Lifeline, the first suicide hotline in the United States dedicated to transgender people.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tona Brown performs at Carnegie Hall
description: Violinist and vocalist Tona Brown became the first African-American openly transgender woman to perform at Carnegie Hall in New York City.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Diego Comic-Con hosts its first transgender panel
description: The Transgender Trends panel at San Diego Comic-Con was the first panel on transgender topics held at the convention.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco police academy graduates first openly trans officer
description: Mikayla Connell became the first openly transgender police officer to graduate from the San Francisco Police academy.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: San Francisco street renamed for trans activist Vicki Marlane
description: The 100 block of Turk Street in San Francisco was renamed Vicki Mar Lane in honor of transgender activist and performer Vicki Marlane.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Lauren Lubin runs New York City Marathon as first openly non-binary athlete
description: Lauren Lubin competed in the New York City Marathon as the race's first openly non-binary athlete.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 21st century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
